Name: 0_bangkok_stay_place_apartment
Representative_Docs:
["I had an amazing stay at Ken.t place in Bangkok! As a first-time traveler, I couldn't have asked for a better location super close to the airport and just a short walk to the BTS train station, making it incredibly easy to explore the city. The building itself is stunning, with top-notch amenities that made my stay so comfortable. Everything was clean, modern, and well-maintained. The host was also very helpful and responsive, making sure I had everything I needed. I highly recommend this place and would definitely stay here again!", "Stop your search!! Do not look any further for a place to stay in Bangkok! IF you want an authentic experience in Bangkok, if you want to stay out of the throngs of tourists, if you want to meet some of the kindest hosts we've ever met, if you want to have beautiful views and peace and quiet (except for the occasional boat going by) to enjoy in the morning before you go out to explore, and if you want to have amazingly delicious breakfasts in the morning, THIS IS the place for you! Our group of 7 stayed at the canal house for 3 nights at the beginning of December, and all of us unanimously agreed that it was a terrific decision. We had the perfect amount of bedrooms to spread out and be together while still having privacy. The 3 bathrooms downstairs together ended up being no issue at all and were very convenient with good water pressure and hot water that did not run out after 2-5 minutes (a rarity we learned while staying in Thailand). The house itself was immaculately clean, and even though it was right on a canal, the windows were all screened in so mosquitos were not an issue. The canal house property is BEAUTIFUL and just so lovely to come home to and enjoy in the morning before heading out for the day. My husband and I would make our way out while everyone was still sleeping or getting ready and enjoy sitting by the canal or lying in the hammock watching the boats go by and reading a book. Our group was a little worried that the location may make it difficult to reach the main sights, but we had absolutely no issue getting Grab taxis back and forth from the canal house and we never felt unsafe returning back home at night. Now, for the hosts themselves - Sirichan's family is AMAZING, and honestly some of the kindest people we ever met!! They were so accommodating, kind, and helpful. The breakfasts prepared by the matriarch were some of the most, if not the most, delicious we had during our entire trip. We miss the canal house, the wonderful family that owns the property, and our time there - next time we are back in Bangkok, we will not hesitate to stay here for one second! Tips: 1) definitely ask for the Mango sticky rice for one of your mornings as others have mentioned - it's delicious!! 2) taking a GRAB to the canal house was a very short wait, but taking a GRAB from the canal house to the city was a longer wait of around 10-15 minutes as it's further from the tourist sites, so plan accordingly. 3) recommend setting up airport pickups and drop-offs through Sirichan to make getting to/from airport easier - it's pricier than GRAB but is much more convenient especially for getting to/from airport on time and efficiently - Mr. T was a wonderful driver! 4) Around 7AM or a bit earlier, boats start cruising down the canal past the house for the morning commute - some have loud motors, so if you sleep late and are easily woken up bring earplugs - the boats were not an issue for any of us, but just a heads up for those who are very noise-sensitive.", "I had an amazing stay at Mr. B apartment! The location is unbeatable - it's close to everything you could want in Bangkok. The host, Mr. B was incredibly hospitable, responsive and made me feel right at home. The room was clean, stylish, comfortable, and had everything I needed for my stay.
But the real highlight was the rooftop swimming pool and gym on the 49th floor - the view was breathtaking 360 degree of Bangkok! It was the perfect place to relax and unwind after a long day of exploring the city.
What really set this apartment was how easy it was to commute around Bangkok. Mister B gave me great tips on how to get around the city and great restaurant and bars in Bangkok.
Overall, I had a fantastic experience and would highly recommend this place to anyone visiting Bangkok. Thanks again to Mister B for making my stay so enjoyable, I wll come back here again whenever I am in Bangkok for sure!"]

Name: 2_apartment_pool_good_room
Representative_Docs:
["Great stay for the cost.
Great living room setup - high quality TV with a comfy sofa.
The wifi is very fast and has ethernet connection.
The washer is new and efficient, and drying my clothes was easy and fast.
Loved how there's a work table and a separate table in the kitchen for dining.
Loved the kitchen - living room - bedroom - bathroom 4 way separation.
2 AC units, one in the bathroom and one in the bedroom.
Had a rice cooker, which really helped.
The kitchen is well stocked, great for cooking.
Great location, no main road traffic noise.
The AC unit in the bedroom is old and makes a loud buzzing sound sometimes.
Not a fan of the bathroom design, not much counter space, shower felt cheap and had low water pressure.
You can sometimes hear your neighbors if they're loud but they're usually quiet. Walls aren't super thick.
There was morning construction some days.
Earplugs recommended.
Alex was very responsive, helpful, and kind. He even showed me some of his other Airbnbs.", 'Had a great stay, the apartment is perfect for a couple, spacious and had everything we needed. Great and stable internet speed, comfortable bed, the kitchen was equiped and made it possible for us to cook and since we had a longer stay, this was really important for us. The amenities are great, the pool is big and is always clean and the building has security at the entrance, all the time. Krittiya was an amazing host, she came by the apartment and explained what we can do around the area, which was a great help. You have a lot of things close by, a grocery store, a KFC, a 7-11 right across the street and a big mall only 5 min away by bus. We really wanted an apartment not close to the city, but one that had great connections if we wanted to go to the city center and the location of this apartment made that possible. Bus station is a few minutes away and you have a lot of options which you can choose from to get to the city center. Thank you again for an amazing stay!', "The apartment is cozy and has everything you need. The gym and swimming pool are a plus and Wi-Fi is very fast.
Unfortunately, I couldn't bring myself to give it 5 stars due to a couple of things that could have been better.
For starters, we were provided only 2 towels (for 2 guests), usually you'd get 2 big and 2 small ones, since I don't want to dry my face with the towel I use to dry my other bits 😅. When I asked for more the communication was a bit odd and somewhat robotic, and anyway in the end I was told to just wash the already provided towels - I was there for more than 1 week.
The place looked mostly clean, but the drain in the bathroom let off a weird smell that spread around the house. The apartment is also next to the garbage room so a few unwanted bugs made their way in through the door.
Like others mentioned the apartment is not well lit in the main area, but it wasn't so bothersome unless I had online work meetings, in which case, I was able to move a table lamp over to light up that corner of the room.
I found it a bit difficult to get to the right building since it's at the very back, and the guard didn't really help.
Lastly, location-wise it is not far from the center, but personally I felt like it was not well connected, and with Bangkok traffic it can still take a while to get anywhere. I also didn't feel so safe walking around that area as soon as it got dark.
All in all I think it's still a good place if you need an apartment with kitchen and amenities, especially if you are alone.
However, for the same price, I would rather book a hotel that is more central and easy to find next time.
I noticed that on Airbnb hosts tend to get a bit feisty if you give negative feedback so I want to make it clear that this is my subjective experience and I still gave 4 stars even though maybe some people would have given less. I don't want to damage the overall score as I think the place is not bad, and maybe others would be totally fine with it. It just needs some polishing and definitely more towels please 🙏"]

Name: 4_bts_condo_station_walk
Representative_Docs:
["The condo is as described. It's small, but clean, cozy and comfortable for couples. We stayed for a month and had everything we needed. Amenities, the bed is comfy, AC and appliances working great.
The building is nice! The pool and gym facilities are good as well. There is a washing/dryer area and you can refill your water bottles as well. There are a couple 7/11 vending machines and a coffee machine.
Location is top!
Next to a 7/11…
5 min walk to a Big C Extra (&mall)
10 min walk to BTS On Nut and Lotus (&mall)…
Tons of restaurants, shops and markets nearby as well.
We would definitely recommend Dana's condo and we hope to come back soon!", "The place overall is great for a long-time stay. It's exactly as in pictures.
It's quite comfortable, has plenty of storage space, a nice view of the inside courtyard with the pool, very chill and relaxing. The sofa in the living room and micro in the kitchen is a bit tired, but it still works.
The location is good, just a bit far from the BTS, but there's a shuttle bus as well as plenty of bike taxi so it's not a problem. There're few restaurants at the area, some massages, a 7-11 literally at the corner of the building and a big supermarket near the BTS.
Small gym and a nice pool at the territory are what makes the place worth every penny.
Generally, great place for the price in the developing area.
And Yin was a responsive and helpful host, thank you again for your hospitality!", "I had a wonderful 7 days at Rachel's place.
Excellent communication from her and she gave very detailed instructions that allowed me to find her place with no trouble at all.
She and her family were very welcoming - even inviting me to share their lovely dinner on my very first night there.
The place is very conveniently located between 2 BTS stations - only a 5-10 minute walk.
Very handy in getting around on the BTS system - and if needed onto the MRT where the two transport systems intersect.
Free hot drinks and water were also supplied daily.
Laundry serviced was also offered at a very reasonable rate.
The room was very clean and all amenities were provided - towels, toothbrushes, soap etc
There's a 7-11 very close by as well as street food and drinks.
I enjoyed my stay there !! Great WiFi speeds too :)
Tip: for cheap and delicious food .. I discovered 'Pier Terminal 21' in the mall at the Asok BTS station on Sukhumvit Road. The food there is actually CHEAPER than some of the street food stalls!! I had a noodles dish for 30 Baht. Amazing value.
There are over 20 food stalls on the 5th floor where you will dine in air conditioned comfort ."]

Name: 6_bkk_place_stay_great
Representative_Docs:
['The place was really nice and clean. Also, closed to the bus station, MRT, 7eleven, and Chinatown night market which is famous for tourists. They are all walking distance food and transportation. It was a great time staying there. The communication with the host is really quick reply and helpful, as well as the staff. Thank you so much for your host and I recommend to stay there to visit Chinatown and around area even central world, etc.', "KB's place is really convenient and close to the BTS. It's about 10 mins walk to the BTS. But there will be a tuktuk at the condo to fetch you to the BTS. The room is really clean and is exactly like how it is shown in the photos. Really comfortable bed plus a huge TV for you to watch while you're on the bed. Water pressure in the bathroom is good too plus bathroom is really clean as well. Overall this is a really nice place to stay and worth the money. Host is really kind and helpful during the stay. I recommend anyone to stay here again if you're looking for a place that is near BTS.", "Small but clean and cozy room near Chinatown. I was here around the Chinese New Year. Loved walking over to Chinatown for food and people-watching several times a day :) Sam Yot MRT is a 20 min walk away. So is Wat Saket. Plenty of street food options nearby. The housekeeper (Nana) is always around and is very helpful. Let her know if you'd like the room to be cleaned. There's laundry service on site. The wifi was excellent. Sound-proofing is thin, much like most of South East Asia. Luckily, I had quiet neighbors. The bathroom walls really need a facelift.
The host communicates well and can be reached easily via Airbnb messages. She gave me very clear instructions about the self-checkin process. Also recommended restaurants and arranged for my bags to be stored after checkout.
Overall, this place is hard to beat at this price and location. Stay here if you wanna explore Chinatown!"]

Name: 7_airbnb_host_apartment_stay
Representative_Docs:
["We had an absolutely wonderful stay at this Airbnb. From the moment we walked in, it felt like a home away from home. The place was spotlessly clean, beautifully maintained, and incredibly comfortable for our entire family. We especially loved spending time on the balcony — it was peaceful, picturesque, and the perfect spot to unwind together.
The host went above and beyond, providing clear, detailed instructions and excellent communication throughout our stay, which made everything seamless and stress-free. The location was also ideal — close to shopping malls and not too far from the hospital, yet tucked away in a quiet and serene neighborhood.
Overall, it was great value for money. Our family made some lovely memories here and we wouldn't hesitate to book again. Highly recommended!", "The stay was comfortable. The home is about 10 minutes walk from the nearest MRT station so quite convenient. The host provided me with cost and details on how to get to the flat so that was helpful.

I never saw the host in person, I was instructed to get the keycard from the building guard, but they didn't know what was going on when i arrived so in the end, I had to wait for the host to call the guards to explain how I was suppose to gain access into the flat. Eventually the guard figured out that a keycard is left in the flat's mailbox and I was able to access the flat with it and a password for the door's number pad that the host provided me to access the flat.

Although the listing shows that 'essential items' is included, no towels and only one roll of toilet paper was provided so had to go out to search and buy my own. A reminder would have been helpful before the trip; I would have prefer to bring my own towels instead of spending the time to find a supermarket (didn't find one until I went to a different MRT station).

The shower was great and the water was plenty hot once I figured out how to get it to work. There wasn't instruction on how to turn on the hot water. It seemed simple at first but turns out turning the dial on the machine didn't do the job, a discreet bar thing underneath had to be pushed up in order to give it power. I was really worry that I would break it or cause problems by doing it, a little instruction on this could have been useful.

Beyond that, the flat was clean with adequate air conditioning. There was only 2 Thai channels that wasn't fuzzy on the TV, luckily I brought my own HDMI cable to watch shows from my computer instead.

The spring mattress was slightly harder than my liking, but I guess that's a personal preference thing. With the building tucked away on a small street, there wasn't any traffic noise or disturbance for a good night rest. The sofa was also very comfortable after a whole day of walking as well.

The area is decent. There is a large size 7-11 just around the corner so good for snacking. Also some street food is available in front of the 7-11.

A night market is setup at a hotel on top of the nearest MRT station. It's not huge but good for some last-minute walk around before heading home to crash. The chatuchuk market and several malls just a few MRT stations away.

Generally and aside from the lack of towel and toilet paper , I was happy with my stay. ", "We had a great stay at this Airbnb! The place is exactly as described! Clean, comfortable, and in a super convenient location. It's just a short walk to the BTS train station and right near a 7-Eleven. The area is lively and full of restaurants, coffee shops, and street food stalls!
Most importantly, Gab, the host, was incredibly helpful and responsive throughout my stay. Excellent service from start to finish. I highly recommend this place and would definitely stay here again. 5 stars all the way!
Thank you Gab ❤️"]
